rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces local property tax revenue for school districts, potentially affecting the neutrality standard of substantially equal access to similar revenue per student at similar tax effort. State funding adjustments would be needed to maintain compliance.
quote: The public school finance system of this state shall adhere to a standard of neutrality that provides for substantially equal access to similar revenue per student at similar tax effort, considering all state and local tax revenues of districts after acknowledging all legitimate student and district cost differences.
_note: §48.001 is a policy declaration, not an operative funding formula; no direct textual conflict but increased homestead exemption pressures the neutrality standard and may require formula adjustments elsewhere in Title 2, Subtitle A, Educ. Code.
